title: Nadine Houle
content:
People.ContactEmail nahoule@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Sherbrooke
Nadine Houle is an associate in our Montréal Labour & Employment Group. She represents employers under both provincial and federal legislation across a wide range of industries.
Nadine’s practice encompasses all aspects of labour and employment law, including hiring, disciplinary measures, complex terminations, workplace discrimination and harassment, human rights issues, restrictive covenants, pay equity, and the interpretation and application of collective bargaining agreements. She also conducts collective bargaining on behalf of employers and provides strategic support on commercial transactions, including corporate restructuring, mergers and acquisitions, and business sales operations.
With a strong background and a particular focus on litigation, Nadine regularly appears before judicial and administrative bodies, including the Administrative Labour Tribunal, arbitration tribunals, the Canada Industrial Relations Board, human rights tribunals, the Québec Superior Court, and the Québec Court of Appeal. She acts for clients in both unionized and non-unionized environments, and has particular experience with urgent and extraordinary remedies, such as obtaining and contesting injunctions and safeguard orders.
Nadine is frequently called upon to deliver practical and educational training for managers and employees, helping organizations stay informed of their legal obligations and best practices in the workplace. She is committed to supporting her clients through proactive advice and effective advocacy.
Nadine was called to the Barreau du Québec in 2021.
